title: 10 best herbal teas for frizzy hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for frizzy hair are infusions made from various herbs that can help improve the condition of frizzy hair. These teas are rich in nutrients, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory properties that can nourish the scalp, strengthen hair follicles, and promote healthy hair growth. Examples of herbal teas that can help with frizzy hair include chamomile, rosemary, nettle, and horsetail. Chamomile tea can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, while rosemary tea can stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth. Nettle tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that can strengthen hair follicles, reducing breakage and frizz. Horsetail tea is high in silica, which can help improve the texture and shine of hair. By using herbal teas for frizzy hair, individuals can achieve smoother, more manageable hair, leading to improved self-confidence and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with frizzy hair because its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and excess oil production. The tea's ability to balance the scalp's natural pH levels also helps to smooth cuticles and tame frizz, leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. Additionally, yarrow's astringent properties help to tighten pores, reducing buildup and flyaways for a silky, smooth finish.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help to tighten and shrink the hair follicles, reducing frizz and adding smoothness to the hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair by causing irritation and damage.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidants help protect the hair from environmental stressors, such as free radicals, that can cause damage and lead to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, roots help to cleanse and purify the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Leaves: Abundant in antioxidants and flavonoids, leaves soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and stems for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine one teaspoon of dried yarrow mixture with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the yarrow mixture in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and properties to meld.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and leaves.
Enjoy the warm, slightly bitter and earthy tea within 15-20 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag teas helps with frizzy hair because it contains a unique compound called calamus oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When used as a tea rinse after shampooing, calamus oil helps to soothe the scalp and calm down frizz-prone strands, leaving hair looking smooth and manageable. The tea's antioxidants also work to nourish and protect the hair follicles, reducing frizz and adding shine to each strand.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Sweet Flag tea's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, which can contribute to frizz by damaging the hair cuticle.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in Sweet Flag tea neutralize free radicals that can damage the hair follicles and cause frizz, leading to smoother and more manageable hair.
Soothing and calming property: The soothing and calming properties of Sweet Flag tea help to calm irritated scalps and reduce stress, which are common triggers for frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and mucilage, which helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Rhyzomes: High in saponins, which can help to cleanse and purify the scalp, reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried acorus calamus roots measure out two to three teaspoons of root material.
Rinse the roots with cold water then chop them into smaller pieces and crush them slightly.
Combine the crushed root material with one cup of boiling water steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with frizzy hair because its natural compounds, such as mucilage, deeply moisturize and nourish dry, damaged locks. The tea's antioxidants and flavonoids work to calm and smooth the cuticle layer of the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the tea's humectant properties help retain moisture, leaving hair soft, silky, and manageable. By regularly drinking marshmallow tea, individuals can experience a significant reduction in frizz and an overall improvement in hair texture and appearance.
remedy_properties: Glycoproteins: Marshmallow root contains glycoproteins, which can help to moisturize and hydrate the hair shaft, reducing frizz and flyaways by locking in moisture.
Mucilage: The mucilage in marshmallow root acts as a protective barrier on the hair cuticle, smoothing out rough ends and preventing dryness and damage that can lead to frizz.
Antioxidants: Marshmallow tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, leading to healthier and smoother-looking hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help to soothe and calm frizzy hair by adding moisture and reducing flyaways.
Bark: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce dandruff and itchiness associated with frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried althaea officinalis leaves and flowers for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herbs or two teaspoons of fresh herbs per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point then reduce heat to simmer for five minutes.
Steep the herb mixture in hot water for an additional seven to ten minutes, covered.
Strain and serve warm or let cool for a soothing, caffeine-free herbal tea drink.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with frizzy hair because it deeply nourishes and moisturizes the hair follicles, leaving your locks soft and manageable. The antioxidants present in burdock root help to reduce inflammation and repair damaged hair cuticles, smoothing out the hair's texture and reducing frizz. Regular consumption of burdock tea can also promote healthy scalp conditions, further enhancing hair health and appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Burdock tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce inflammation that can contribute to frizz.

Saponins: Saponins in burdock tea can help strengthen hair follicles by increasing blood flow to the scalp and promoting healthy hair growth, reducing frizz and flyaways caused by weak or damaged hair.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in burdock tea have been shown to improve hair texture and reduce frizz by enhancing the natural moisture barrier of the hair shaft, making it less prone to dryness and brittleness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps balance scalp pH and reduce dandruff, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which nourish the hair follicles and strengthen the hair shaft, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried burdock root per cup of water for optimal infusion.
Boil fresh filtered water in a pot or teapot and let it cool down slightly.
Add the dried burdock root to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or carafe.
Serve the herbal tea hot or chilled depending on personal preference within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with frizzy hair because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. These properties help to balance the scalp's pH levels, reducing inflammation and calming irritated skin. As a result, wormwood tea can help to tame frizz by reducing excess oil production and promoting healthy scalp function. Additionally, wormwood's ability to strengthen hair follicles may also contribute to its hair-smoothing effects, leaving hair looking silky and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wormwood tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals in the scalp and hair follicles, reducing oxidative stress and damage to the hair structure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation and redness on the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair by damaging the cuticle layer of the hair shaft.

Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent compounds help to tighten and constrict the pores on the scalp, reducing sebum production and minimizing excess oil buildup that can lead to frizz and unmanageable hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, wormwood roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz and adding shine to unruly locks.
Bark: Containing tannins and flavonoids, the bark of the wormwood plant helps tighten and firm the hair follicles, reducing frizz and flyaways while promoting a smoother, more manageable texture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves and stems of artemisia absinthium in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse 10-15 grams of dried wormwood material with boiling water to remove any impurities.
Steep the wormwood material in 500ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool and settle before serving, which may take around 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch teas helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in antioxidants that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe an irritated scalp, which can contribute to frizzy locks. Additionally, silver birch contains salicin, a natural alpha-hydroxy acid that gently exfoliates the hair shaft, removing impurities and product buildup that can cause frizz. As a result, drinking silver birch tea as part of a regular beauty routine can leave hair looking healthy, smooth, and silky.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have been shown to improve skin and hair health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to frizziness.
Tannins: Tannins in silver birch tea act as a natural astringent, helping to tighten and smooth the cuticle of the hair shaft, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Silica: Silica is an essential mineral that helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve the overall structure of the hair, making it less prone to frizz and breakage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to clarify and detangle hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Bark: Contains salicin, a natural beta hydroxy acid that helps to regulate sebum production and add shine to dry, frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silver birch leaves, using scissors or a knife, for a total of 20-30 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 1-2 hours, or air-dry them for several days.
Combine the dried silver birch leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, adding 250ml of water per 10g of leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking, consuming within 24 hours of preparation and refrigerating any leftovers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with frizzy hair because its antioxidants and flavonoids work to strengthen hair follicles, reduce split ends, and tame unruly strands. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe the scalp, reducing irritation that can contribute to frizz. Additionally, the calcium and magnesium in pot marigold tea help nourish and moisturize the hair shaft, leaving it soft, smooth, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair by disrupting the hair follicles' natural growth cycle.
Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Pot marigold tea's antibacterial and antifungal agents help to combat scalp infections and irritation that can lead to frizz, itchiness, and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties in roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz-causing inflammation.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and vitamins, leaves help nourish and protect the hair follicles, promoting healthy and smooth locks.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the dried calendula flowers with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to ten minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or tea strainer and discard the solids.
Enjoy the warm or cooled herbal tea as is or add honey to taste and sweeten.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with frizzy hair because of its high antioxidant content, which helps to smooth and calm the cuticle layer of the hair. The catechins in white tea also help to strengthen and repair damaged hair follicles, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the soothing properties of white tea can help to reduce irritation and inflammation on the scalp, leading to a healthier and more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea is rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and breakage.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like catechins and quercetin, which can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation that contributes to frizzy hair.
Tannins: White tea's tannin content helps to strengthen the hair cuticle, reducing porosity and allowing less moisture to escape, resulting in smoother, less frizzy locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots can help soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Bark: Containing natural astringents and toners, bark helps to balance the scalp's pH and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of high-quality camellia sinensis leaves per cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 150-160°f within 5 minutes.
Steep the leaves in hot water for 2-3 minutes or until desired strength.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot.
Serve the herbal white tea immediately and enjoy within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with frizzy hair because of its ability to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing frizz and flyaways. The tea's antioxidant properties also help to repair damaged cuticles, leaving hair smooth and manageable. Additionally, caraway tea can help to tighten the hair follicles, reducing shedding and promoting healthy hair growth. By consuming this tea regularly, individuals with frizzy hair may experience a significant reduction in frizz and an improvement in overall hair texture.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Caraway tea contains antioxidants that help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness, promoting healthier and smoother locks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in caraway tea may help to soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation, which can cause frizzy hair, while also reducing redness and irritation.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Caraway tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help to control the growth of fungi and bacteria on the scalp, preventing conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis that can lead to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, caraway roots help reduce frizz and add shine to dry, damaged hair.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, caraway seeds nourish and moisturize the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried caraway seeds for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a tea kettle or pot to a rolling boil.
Add the measured caraway seeds to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to blend.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola teas helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and moisturize the scalp and hair follicles. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe dryness and irritation, reducing frizz-causing damage. Additionally, gotu kola's ability to improve blood circulation stimulates healthy hair growth, resulting in stronger, smoother strands that resist frizz and flyaways, leaving you with a sleeker, more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Gotu kola tea helps to reduce frizz by tightening the cuticle layer of the hair, reducing moisture loss and adding shine to dry hair.
Anti-inflammatory property: Gotu kola's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce irritation caused by product buildup or styling damage, leading to a reduction in frizz.
Antioxidant property: Gotu kola tea's antioxidants help protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, Gotu kola roots help to calm the scalp and reduce frizz by soothing irritation and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the plant, rhyzomes contain antioxidants that help to repair damaged hair cuticles and reduce frizz by protecting against environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried gotu kola leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it over the gotu kola leaves in the tea infuser or cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea.
Strain the tea into another cup using the tea infuser or by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve lined with cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature and consume within 24 hours of preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frizzy hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled, brewed tea. Chamomile tea adds shine and silkiness to dry hair, while green tea seals in moisture and adds luster. For a strengthening treatment, use rosemary tea, which is also known to promote hair growth. After shampooing, pour the tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp. Let it sit for a few minutes before rinsing with cold water to seal the cuticle and add shine. Regular use of herbal tea rinses can help combat frizz, leaving you with smooth, manageable hair.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and dry, itchy scalp. Certain herbal teas, like chamomile and nettle, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can promote hair growth and reduce hair loss. Peppermint tea can help to stimulate the scalp and improve blood circulation, which can lead to stronger, healthier hair. These herbal teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or steeped and applied directly to the scalp. However, it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
